candidate_articles.title: Senate Passes Clean Energy Innovation Act in Bipartisan Vote
candidate_articles.snippet: The Senate voted 62-38 Thursday to advance the Clean Energy Innovation Act, sending the bill to the House amid renewed bipartisan interest in climate legislation.
candidate_articles.reason: Article is directly about the bill, covering vote and Congressional action

candidate_articles.title: Solar Panel Costs Fall 90% in a Decade — What Comes Next?
candidate_articles.snippet: The dramatic drop in solar costs has outpaced all projections. Next-generation perovskite cells and tandem architectures could push efficiency beyond current silicon limits.
candidate_articles.reason: Directly covers subject matter (solar technology) that the bill funds, helps user understand context

candidate_articles.title: Battery Storage Projects Hit Record Investment in 2023
candidate_articles.snippet: Global battery storage installations hit 42 GWh last year, triple 2021 levels, driven by falling costs and grid reliability concerns.
candidate_articles.reason: Battery storage is a key area funded by the bill; gives user market context

candidate_articles.title: Local Election Results: City Council Picks New Mayor

candidate_articles.title: Critics Say Clean Energy Bill Favors Big Utilities Over Rooftop Solar
candidate_articles.snippet: Clean energy advocates split on the bill, with distributed energy groups arguing research funding flows primarily to utility-scale projects at the expense of residential solar.
candidate_articles.reason: Critical perspective on the bill — important for user to form balanced opinion

candidate_articles.title: Rural Hospital in Georgia to Close, Leaving County Without Emergency Care
candidate_articles.snippet: St. James Hospital in rural Telfair County will close its doors April 1, the latest in a string of rural hospital closures that has accelerated since 2020.
candidate_articles.reason: Illustrates the real-world problem the bill is trying to solve — emotionally resonant context

candidate_articles.title: Senate Debates Rural Healthcare Act Amid Hospital Closure Crisis
candidate_articles.snippet: The Senate HELP Committee heard testimony from rural hospital administrators and patient advocates as it weighs the Rural Healthcare Access Act.
candidate_articles.reason: Direct coverage of the bill in committee

candidate_articles.title: Study: Rural Americans Have 30% Higher Mortality Rate for Preventable Conditions
candidate_articles.snippet: A new JAMA study finds rural Americans die at significantly higher rates from heart disease, stroke, and diabetes than urban counterparts, largely due to delayed care.
candidate_articles.reason: Statistical evidence supporting the public health case for the bill

candidate_articles.title: EU AI Act Takes Effect — What US Companies Must Know
candidate_articles.snippet: European AI regulation took effect this month, creating compliance obligations for any company deploying high-risk AI systems in the EU — including many US tech firms.
candidate_articles.reason: International comparison context helps user understand the regulatory landscape the US bill is responding to

candidate_articles.title: AI Accountability Act Introduced in Senate with Bipartisan Support
candidate_articles.snippet: Senators from both parties introduced the AI Accountability Act, marking the first major federal AI regulation bill to advance out of committee.
candidate_articles.reason: Direct reporting on the bill's introduction and political dynamics

candidate_articles.title: Facial Recognition Wrongly Identified 6 Innocent People, ACLU Report Finds
candidate_articles.snippet: A new report documents six cases where facial recognition technology used by law enforcement misidentified innocent individuals, leading to wrongful arrests.
candidate_articles.reason: Real-world AI harm case illustrates the risks the bill's accountability provisions target
